microsoft_ft: Owned end-to-end program strategy for Azure's resilience validation platform, driving **45+ annual drills** across enterprise partners (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp) and delivering **$14M+ business impact** by standardizing cross-org execution workflows.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining extensibility requirements and self-service scheduling primitives that reduced operational toil by **30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, self-service model.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility — resolving hard ownership questions across DRI boundaries and serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a repeatable model for continuous platform validation.

Drove program-level roadmap influence by advocating for cross-cutting investments no single team would self-fund, reducing planning cycle time by **39%** and increasing drill capacity by 21% through an internal AI agent and restructured workflows.
microsoft_2023: Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** by conducting 11+ user interviews with Azure service teams, surfacing critical API and developer-experience gaps that no individual team had prioritized.

Championed adoption of AI-driven code generation workflows across **14 key engineering teams**, running demos and training sessions that saved 37 engineering hours monthly and accelerated platform utilization.

Improved developer-platform data integrity by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting lookup time by 83% across internal SDK consumers.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, launching regions 28% faster and generating **$3M in accelerated revenue** by resolving competing roadmap priorities at the program level.

Mobilized a platform strategy to eliminate launch toil, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to map **81 hours of manual effort per region** and building a roadmap to automate critical-path workflows across the extensibility layer.

Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps and direct cross-org investment toward the highest-impact platform improvements.
amazon_robotics: Defined the legacy OS migration strategy and mapped dependencies across **1,200+ stations**, achieving zero operational downtime during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by driving alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering DRIs.

Drove cross-org alignment to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, resolving integration and ownership ambiguities between platform and product teams and accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25%.
Facilitated Agile ceremonies and owned backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and unblock downstream program execution.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflow for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a CRM process that standardized cross-functional execution between sales and operations.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web and search surfaces to align platform presence with enterprise customer needs.

Identified and sealed margin leaks by conducting financial profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records**, delivering pricing model improvements that gave leadership a data-driven basis for roadmap and investment decisions.

tailoring_notes: 'extensibility layer' woven into microsoft_2022 bullet 2 — mirrors JD's core program name and platform framing.

'DRI boundaries' and 'ownership questions' used in microsoft_ft bullet 3 — directly mirrors JD language: 'resolving the hard, ambiguous integration and ownership questions that fall between team boundaries.'
'self-service primitives' used in microsoft_ft bullet 2 — mirrors JD's 'extensibility primitives' (Scripts, Custom Objects, Workflows) framing to signal platform fluency.

'cross-org roadmap influence' and 'cross-cutting investments no single team would self-fund' in microsoft_ft bullet 5 — mirrors JD requirement: 'advocating for cross-cutting investments that no single team will prioritize on their own.'
'developer-experience gaps' and 'SDK consumers' in microsoft_2023 bullet 1 — mirrors JD's DEeP org focus on developer experience and SDK surfaces.
